title: Breast21 First Customer Meeting
subtitle: Pathologists & Oncologists · Export Markets
purpose: Understand the account's current behaviour and decision criteria, qualify it, position Breast21 against the criteria they state, and secure one concrete next step.

questions.prompt: Are you currently using a recurrence-risk / genomic signature test for early-stage HR+/HER2 breast cancer?
questions.help: After a couple of introduction questions, show the Breast21 features slide. This answer determines the interview branch.

options.label: Yes, but only for few patients

questions.prompt: Which recurrence-risk test(s) are you using, and since when?
questions.help: Capture Oncotype DX / MammaPrint / EndoPredict / Prosigna / other. Ask whether different tests are used for different situations, e.g. lobular carcinoma.

questions.prompt: Why was this specific test chosen over alternatives?
questions.help: Probe clinical evidence, guideline inclusion, price, reimbursement and turnaround time.

questions.prompt: Who is typically involved in selecting and approving the use of a recurrence-risk test?
Approximately how many patients receive recurrence-risk testing annually at this institution?
Are all eligible patients tested, or are there limitations or subpopulations?
questions.help: Examples: no reimbursement, local guidelines, only specific patient profiles.

questions.prompt: What limitations, frustrations, or barriers do you experience with your current testing approach?
What does the test cost, who pays, and what percentage of tested patients are reimbursed?
questions.help: Capture government, private insurance, hospital budget, patient out-of-pocket, or mixed models.

questions.prompt: Pathologist optional: does your lab have experience with micro- or macrodissection when needed?
questions.help: Specimen considerations mentioned in the guide: tumor tissue input, neoplastic cell content, removal of structures such as biopsy cavity or DCIS when needed.

questions.section: Branch B — They are not testing, or very few patients
questions.prompt: Why are recurrence-risk tests not used, or used only for very few patients?
questions.help: Keep this open. If they name one barrier, ask what other barriers exist.

questions.section: Branch B — They are not testing, or very few patients
questions.prompt: Which barriers are most relevant?

questions.section: Branch B — They are not testing, or very few patients
questions.prompt: Is there institutional funding that could cover an in-house test even where it would not cover a send-out test?

questions.section: Branch B — They are not testing, or very few patients
questions.prompt: If barriers were solved, approximately how many eligible patients per year could be tested?

questions.section: Branch B — They are not testing, or very few patients
questions.prompt: Who would need to be involved to establish or approve recurrence-risk testing?
What minimum evidence would you need to implement the Breast21 Assay in your hospital?
questions.help: Show the slide on planned studies: concordance study and validation study proving prognostic value.

questions.prompt: Biocartis is aiming to demonstrate >90% concordance between Idylla Breast21 and Oncotype DX. Would this level be sufficient to support consideration of adoption?
What additional evidence or data would be required?
Which factors would most strongly influence your decision to switch to the Idylla™ Breast21 Test?
If Idylla Breast21 were initially available as a Research Use Only assay, how would that affect your interest?
Would your institution consider validating a RUO assay as a Laboratory Developed Test for clinical use? Why or why not?
Summarize: what regulatory status is required for adoption?
Summarize: what level of evidence is needed to support adoption?
Summarize: what is the minimum concordance percentage they require?
Summarize: what would be an acceptable or ideal price range?
Do they have additional questions?
questions.help: If a question is not covered by the FAQ, save it also in 'Questions to Share'.

questions.prompt: What concrete next step was agreed before leaving?
options: Follow-up meeting once concordance data is available

questions.prompt: Who else in the institution should be in the next conversation?
Final notes / meeting summary

fieldNotes: In several markets the two/three-week Oncotype DX turnaround is genuinely a non-issue.
If an oncologist will not switch without prospective data, near-term adoption will not happen. Site will likely wait until IVD availability.
